skill.name: check-plan
skill.description: Audit implementation progress against a plan, verify completed work, identify gaps, and produce actionable remaining tasks.
skill.summary: Checks whether implementation work fully satisfies a plan and its original requirements.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: AI review found no confirmed malicious intent, prompt injection, credential theft, or network exfiltration. The static weak-cryptography and Ruby backtick alerts are false positives from Markdown prose and templates. The skill does require repository file access and local command execution, so it should publish with a medium-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run local commands such as git status, git diff, grep, gh issue view, npm scripts, tsc, eslint, and build commands. This is legitimate for a code audit, but it can execute project-defined scripts and inspect repository history.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and context confirms they are intended to be run during audits. The commands are common developer commands, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Broad Repository Read and Report Writing
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the agent to read plan files, inspect affected source files, and create a progress report under .plans. This is expected behavior for the skill, but it requires repository file access and may expose sensitive project content to the model.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions clearly require reading local project files and writing a generated report. The behavior is disclosed and aligned with the skill purpose, which limits severity.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Blocks
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick detections are Markdown code fences and inline examples, not Ruby code execution. No evidence found of bundled executable code or dynamic command construction in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are Markdown examples and report templates. There is no Ruby source file, no interpreter invocation, and no runtime backtick expression.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary prose, Markdown headings, placeholders, or status text. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed context shows planning and report-template text only. The apparent crypto matches do not correspond to cryptographic APIs or algorithm names used for security.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance alert points to the supporting-tool list, not to network scanning behavior. No evidence found of port scanning, host discovery, remote endpoints, or data exfiltration instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited section only lists tools such as Grep, Glob, Bash, Read, TodoWrite, and Memory/Pinboard. It contains no network targets or reconnaissance workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Command Execution Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to run local commands such as git status, git diff, grep, gh issue view, npm scripts, tsc, eslint, and build commands. This is legitimate for a code audit, but it can execute project-defined scripts and inspect repository history.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and context confirms they are intended to be run during audits. The commands are common developer commands, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: Broad Repository Read and Report Writing
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs the agent to read plan files, inspect affected source files, and create a progress report under .plans. This is expected behavior for the skill, but it requires repository file access and may expose sensitive project content to the model.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instructions clearly require reading local project files and writing a generated report. The behavior is disclosed and aligned with the skill purpose, which limits severity.

content.value_statement: Implementation plans can drift from the original request and leave hidden gaps. This skill checks plan coverage, verifies completed work, and produces a clear remaining-task list.
actual_capabilities: Reads a plan from .plans and maps requirements to changed files.
Uses git status, diff, and log to understand implementation scope.
Verifies removal specifications and flags old code that still exists.
Checks original issue coverage against plan steps and implementation.
Generates progress reports with completion status and remaining tasks.
Recommends validation checks such as typecheck, lint, and build.
limitations: Requires access to the plan, repository files, and local tooling.
Does not implement missing work; it audits and reports only.
Validation accuracy depends on project scripts and environment setup.
GitHub issue lookup requires a configured GitHub CLI and permissions.

use_cases.title: Review feature completion before handoff
use_cases.description: Compare completed code against the approved plan and identify remaining tasks before review.

use_cases.title: Verify acceptance criteria coverage
use_cases.description: Check that original issue requirements are implemented, not only the written plan steps.

use_cases.title: Audit cleanup and migration work
use_cases.description: Confirm old files, deprecated symbols, and removal-spec items were actually deleted.

prompt_templates.title: Check a plan status
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one plan file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use check-plan to review .plans/my-feature.todo.md and tell me what is complete, partial, or not started.

prompt_templates.title: Verify changed files against a plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use check-plan to compare the current branch changes with the plan in .plans/refactor.todo.md. Include scope creep and missing work.

prompt_templates.title: Audit original issue coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use check-plan to verify that issue 128 is fully covered by the implementation and the plan. Flag any requirements missing from either source.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a release readiness report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use check-plan to create a progress report for .plans/api-migration.todo.md. Verify removal specs, run validation checks, and produce prioritized remaining tasks.

output_examples.input: Check whether the authentication refactor plan is complete.
output_examples.output: A concise progress summary showing completed, partial, and missing plan steps, plus high-priority blockers before the plan can be marked done.

output_examples.input: Verify that old billing code was removed after a migration.
output_examples.output: A removal-spec section listing deleted items, remaining legacy references, and exact follow-up tasks for cleanup.

output_examples.input: Review this branch against the original GitHub issue.
output_examples.output: A coverage table summary that compares issue requirements with plan steps and implementation status, followed by validation results.

best_practices: Provide the exact plan file and original issue when available.
Run the skill on a clean branch so git context is easy to interpret.
Review generated remaining tasks before marking a plan as complete.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for tests, type checks, or human review.
Do not mark a plan complete when original requirements remain uncovered.
Do not run project scripts unless you understand their local side effects.

faq.question: Does this skill change source code?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to audit implementation progress and report gaps, not to implement fixes.

faq.question: Can it create files?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow can create a progress report under .plans when requested by the audit process.

faq.question: Why does it use git commands?
faq.answer: Git status, diff, and log help identify changed files and compare them with the plan scope.

faq.question: Does it need the original issue?
faq.answer: It works without one, but original issue details improve coverage checks and reduce missed requirements.

faq.question: Can it verify deleted code?
faq.answer: Yes. It searches for removal-spec items and flags old code, files, or references that still exist.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: Use normal caution. The skill reads repository content and may run local commands during validation.
